key_insight: Replacing the DFTB pairwise repulsive term with a many-body MACE correction yields a DFTB+MACE model that improves accuracy while preserving electronic-structure output.
engineering_relevance: This hybrid approach extends simulation reach for magnesium compounds to larger systems and longer times than DFT, with useful accuracy for structure, dynamics, and phonons.
method: Train MACE on DFT–DFTB energy/force residuals, excluding the pair-potential contribution.
Embed the learned many-body term into a DFTB framework to replace the conventional repulsive potential.
Validate on MgO–CO2 adsorption, water-cluster MD, and fcc/bcc MgO phonons, comparing against DFT and pure MACE.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports improved agreement over DFTB with pair potentials across structural relaxation, molecular dynamics, and phonon calculations, with only moderate added cost.
watch_item: Performance may be limited by the original MACE training set coverage, so unexplored regions of the potential-energy surface could require dataset expansion.
paper_title: A Combined Tight Binding with Machine Learning Potential Model for Magnesium Compounds
